intro: Prosthechea sceptra, also known as the "sword orchid," is a species of epiphytic orchid native to Central and South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce fever. The bioactive constituents of Prosthechea sceptra include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include tinctures, extracts, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including respiratory issues and skin conditions.
